ogTitle: Sandy Gully vs Scaddan: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Sandy Gully and Scaddan.
heading: Sandy Gully vs Scaddan — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Sandy Gully, Western Australia and Scaddan, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Sandy Gully has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Scaddan (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Sandy Gully or Scaddan safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Sandy Gully has a flood risk score of 37/100, while Scaddan scores 55/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Sandy Gully scores 46/100 for bushfire risk. Scaddan scores 37/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Sandy Gully or Scaddan?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Sandy Gully has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.6% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
